steps.title: Find the slope between two points
steps.content:
Using points from the table: $(-6, 65)$ and $(6, 29)$:
$$\text{slope} = \frac{29 - 65}{6 - (-6)} = \frac{-36}{12} = -3$$
The relationship has a slope of $-3$.

steps.title: Test a point in the answer choices
steps.content:
Substitute the point $(6, 29)$ into each choice:
  • A: $9(6) + 3(29) = 54 + 87 = 141$ ✓
  • B: $9(6) + 3(29) = 141$, but $141 eq 3$ ✗
  • C: $3(6) + 9(29) = 18 + 261 = 279 eq 141$ ✗
  • D: $3(6) + 9(29) = 18 + 261 = 279 eq 3$ ✗
Only choice A satisfies the point. That matches choice A — A is correct.
